meta.title: Code Retrieval & Enterprise Codebases
meta.sub: Literature explorer · 85 papers, 5 themes · SciX MCP (arXiv + ADS) + industry sources · built 2026-06-08
meta.what_is: A map of code-retrieval and code-search research with an emphasis on what breaks once you leave the single-file demo and enter a living, multi-million-line enterprise codebase. Built via SciX MCP hybrid search plus sub-agent deep-dives, cross-referenced against industrial code-search blogs (Kythe, Zoekt, Glean, SCIP) and three local benchmark projects (EnterpriseBench, CodeScaleBench, codeprobe).
meta.caveat: The dense/ANN index was down during source gathering, so recall here is lexical/hybrid only — some named systems (RepoFusion, RepoGraph, GRACE) did not surface. Industrial systems (Kythe / Zoekt / Glean / SCIP) are blog-grounded, not peer-reviewed. Several named benchmarks (published EnterpriseBench arXiv:2510.27287, SWE-bench Pro, Long Code Arena, Gemini 1.5 long-context) are referenced in the deep-dives but absent from this corpus and are therefore described in panels rather than listed as papers.
start_here: ★ Reading path. Start with Why Code Isn't Text IR (CodeSearchNet, CodeBERT, the vocabulary-mismatch papers), then walk the technique ladder (lexical → neural → graph → rerank), then scale up to repository-level retrieval and code graphs, and finish on the enterprise reality and open frontier.

★ Opportunities. The 12 open problems from the brainstorm — access-control-aware retrieval, contamination-proof eval, staleness gating, symbolic+semantic fusion at scale — are in the Opportunities tab; the thinnest academically are permission-bounded retrieval and tribal-knowledge retrieval.

Design deep-dives. The Design tab dissects three keystones: a benchmark family (CodeSearchNet/CoIR), a graph-localization system (LocAgent), and a local enterprise benchmark (EnterpriseBench) — task design, evidence, and what is reusable.
Timeline. The Temporal tab traces the maturation from 2016 NL→API lineage through the 2020 CodeBERT explosion to the 2024-2026 agentic / graph / enterprise wave.
Themes (left nav). Five themes: why code is not text IR, retrieval techniques, repository-scale & code graphs, enterprise challenges, and the open frontier.
meta.one_paragraph: Code retrieval began as text IR with a different corpus and immediately hit a wall: queries and code share little vocabulary, code's lexicon is open-ended, and meaning lives in symbols, references, and structure that a bag-of-tokens embedding flattens away. The field answered with a ladder — lexical/trigram (rare-token recall), dense neural embeddings (intent), AST/graph methods (behavior), and retrieve-then-rerank cascades — that is additive, not a sequence of replacements. Scaling to repositories turned retrieval into navigation: iterated similarity RAG, queryable code graphs, and agentic localization. And scaling to the enterprise changed which problems even exist — billions of LOC, polyglot cross-language dependency chains, access-control boundaries, dead code and churn, tribal knowledge outside the source, and an evaluation crisis where ground truth is contaminated or tautological. The frontier is now an infrastructure problem: a fused symbolic+semantic index that stays fresh under O(changes) updates, gates on freshness and permissions, and is measured honestly.
meta.podcast: Companion to the five-episode Code Intel Digest — Code Retrieval & Enterprise Codebases deep-dive series (Why Code Isn't Text IR; Techniques: Lexical → Neural → Graph; Repository-Scale & Code Graphs; The Unique Challenges of Large Enterprise Codebases; Frontier & Open Problems).
meta.design_sub: Deep dives into keystone systems and benchmarks: design, evidence, and what is reusable.

papers.title: CodeSearchNet Challenge: Evaluating the State of Semantic Code Search

notes.k: Foundational NL→code retrieval benchmark, built around the query/code vocabulary gap
notes.r: Defined the shared yardstick and named the core reason text IR fails on code: query and result rarely share words

papers.title: CodeBERT: A Pre-Trained Model for Programming and Natural Languages

notes.k: Seminal bimodal PL/NL encoder; pretrain on (comment, code) pairs then retrieve
notes.r: The recipe most later embedding models inherit, and the token-level baseline the structure-aware successors reacted to

papers.title: GraphCodeBERT: Pre-training Code Representations with Data Flow

notes.k: Injects data-flow (where-the-value-comes-from) structure with graph-guided masked attention
notes.r: First clean win for structure over tokens; model shifts attention to structure-level edges in code search, SOTA on four tasks

papers.title: CodeT5: Identifier-aware Unified Pre-trained Encoder-Decoder Models for Code

notes.k: Identifier-aware unified encoder-decoder for code understanding and generation
notes.r: Part of the CodeBERT→GraphCodeBERT→CodeT5/UniXcoder embedding-lane lineage

papers.title: UniXcoder: Unified Cross-Modal Pre-training for Code Representation

notes.k: Unified cross-modal embeddings spanning code, comment, and AST
notes.r: Consolidates the encoder lineage into one cross-modal representation for search and generation

papers.title: CoIR: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Code Information Retrieval

notes.k: Comprehensive multi-task, multi-language code-IR benchmark; modern successor to CodeSearchNet
notes.r: Consolidates a fragmented evaluation landscape into one suite so cross-paper scores become comparable

papers.title: CoSQA+: Enhancing Code Search Dataset with Matching Code

notes.k: Code-search eval built from genuine developer web-search queries, not synthetic docstrings
notes.r: Tests the real intent→code gap with queries drawn from search logs

papers.title: Dataflow-Guided Retrieval Augmentation for Repository-Level Code Completion (DraCo)

notes.k: Parses a private repo into a dataflow context graph and retrieves from it at completion time
notes.r: Bridges similarity and graph camps: static analysis steers retrieval (+3.43% EM, +3.27% identifier-F1 on ReccEval)

papers.title: CodeRAG-Bench: Can Retrieval Augment Code Generation?

notes.k: Benchmark isolating whether retrieval actually augments code generation
notes.r: Makes RAG-for-code a measurable, controlled question rather than an assumption

papers.title: CoRNStack: High-Quality Contrastive Data for Better Code Retrieval and Reranking

notes.k: Curated contrastive data and hard negatives for both the retriever and the reranker
notes.r: The data that makes code retrievers/rerankers competitive; metrics unverified via live lookup

papers.title: Deep API Learning

notes.k: Early NL→API sequence learning, ancestor of the DeepCS neural-code-search lineage
notes.r: Marks the pre-CodeBERT origin of learning the intent→code mapping directly

papers.title: Neural Code Search Revisited: Enhancing Code Snippet Retrieval through Natural Language Intent

notes.k: Revisits query-by-NL code snippet retrieval, foregrounding natural-language intent
notes.r: Sharpens the intent-to-snippet matching problem the embedding lane targets

papers.title: deGraphCS: Embedding Variable-based Flow Graph for Neural Code Search

notes.k: Builds a variable-based flow graph and embeds it for code search
notes.r: Graph-based rung: behavior via flow edges that token models cannot see

papers.title: GraphSearchNet: Enhancing GNNs via Capturing Global Dependencies for Semantic Code Search

notes.k: GNNs capture global program dependencies for semantic code search
notes.r: Structure rung that reaches beyond local context to whole-program relations

papers.title: Deep Graph Matching and Searching for Semantic Code Retrieval

notes.k: Reframes retrieval itself as graph matching rather than vector cosine
notes.r: Shows the matching primitive can be structural, not just embedding-distance

papers.title: Opportunities and Challenges in Code Search Tools (CodeMatcher)

notes.k: A lexical method exploiting sequential semantics of query words that rivals early neural search
notes.r: The load-bearing 'strong baseline' result: exact/lexical matching never went away and owns rare-identifier recall

papers.title: Cascaded Fast and Slow Models for Efficient Semantic Code Search

notes.k: Fast bi-encoder for recall paired with a slow classifier for precision
notes.r: Canonical code-specific retrieve-then-rerank cascade

papers.title: Contrastive Code Representation Learning (ContraCode)

notes.k: Learns code representations by pulling functionally-equivalent variants together
notes.r: Contrastive lineage that sharpens embedding quality beyond CodeBERT

papers.title: Code Search: A Survey of Techniques for Finding Code

notes.k: Survey mapping the lexical-vs-neural-vs-hybrid code-search taxonomy
notes.r: The landscape map for the whole technique ladder

papers.title: Detecting Code Clones with Graph Neural Network and Flow-Augmented Abstract Syntax Tree

notes.k: GNN over a flow-augmented AST for clone/similarity detection
notes.r: The clone/similarity primitive underlying behavioral code retrieval

papers.title: TranS^3: A Transformer-based Framework for Unifying Code Summarization and Code Search

notes.k: Unifies code summarization and code search in one neural/hybrid framework
notes.r: Couples the summarize and search tasks that share representation needs

papers.title: ColBERT: Efficient and Effective Passage Search via Contextualized Late Interaction over BERT

notes.k: Late interaction: encode query/doc independently, score with cheap per-token MaxSim
notes.r: The middle ground between bi- and cross-encoders; ~2 orders faster, ~4 orders fewer FLOPs than full reranking, adapted to code

papers.title: Text and Code Embeddings by Contrastive Pre-Training

notes.k: Large-scale contrastive pre-training yields embeddings that transfer to code search
notes.r: Shows general contrastive embeddings reach code; exposes the single-vector no-interaction ceiling

papers.title: Unifying the Perspectives of NLP and Software Engineering: A Survey on Language Models for Code

notes.k: Survey explicitly unifying NLP and SE perspectives on code language models
notes.r: States the series premise: code retrieval borrows from text IR but must diverge

papers.title: CodeBLEU: a Method for Automatic Evaluation of Code Synthesis

notes.k: Injects AST syntax and data-flow semantics into BLEU for evaluating code
notes.r: Proves even measuring code can't borrow text-IR metrics unchanged; correlates better with programmer judgments

papers.title: Big Code != Big Vocabulary: Open-Vocabulary Models for Source Code

notes.k: Names and mechanizes code's open, ever-growing vocabulary (OOV) problem
notes.r: Mechanical root cause of why off-the-shelf text embeddings choke on code identifiers

papers.title: MISIM: A Neural Code Semantics Similarity System (Machine Inferred Code Similarity)

notes.k: Context-aware semantics structure that lifts meaning out of syntax for similarity
notes.r: Clean named instance of structure beating token-bag; existing methods 'mainly operate on identifiers'

papers.title: A Systematic Review of Automated Query Reformulations in Source Code Search

notes.k: Synthesizes 70 studies on rewriting developers' ad-hoc keyword queries
notes.r: That this subfield exists is direct evidence the intent→query→code chain is lossy at every hop

papers.title: CoCoSoDa: Effective Contrastive Learning for Code Search

notes.k: Soft data augmentation plus momentum-queue negatives for NL→code contrastive learning
notes.r: Names the two levers (augmentation, hard negatives) that make code embeddings good

papers.title: RocketQA: An Optimized Training Approach to Dense Passage Retrieval

notes.k: Cross-batch negatives, denoised hard-negative mining, and augmentation for dual encoders
notes.r: Explains why the code-embedding lane needs hard-negative mining: garbage negatives yield a garbage retriever

papers.title: Unsupervised Dense Information Retrieval with Contrastive Learning (Contriever)

notes.k: Unsupervised dense retrieval still loses to BM25 without supervision
notes.r: The inconvenient evidence for 'don't drop lexical' and the foundational argument for hybrid over pure-dense

papers.title: Pretrained Transformers for Text Ranking: BERT and Beyond

notes.k: The multi-stage ranking map: cheap first-stage retriever feeds an expensive reranker
notes.r: Frames the bi- vs cross-encoder vs late-interaction design space the code rung instantiates

papers.title: Heterogeneous Program Graph Representations for Source Code

notes.k: Typed heterogeneous program graphs recover node-type info that plain ASTs discard
notes.r: Concretizes 'why graph beats sequence': control/data-flow edges encode behavior tokens cannot

papers.title: RepoCoder: Repository-Level Code Completion Through Iterative Retrieval and Generation

notes.k: Retrieve-generate-retrieve loop where each draft becomes a better query
notes.r: Canonical iterated-similarity move for cross-file completion, but inherits vector RAG's blindness to structure

papers.title: RepoBench: Benchmarking Repository-Level Code Auto-Completion Systems

notes.k: Establishes repo-level / cross-file completion as a measurable task
notes.r: Exposes how badly single-file models do when the answer lives in another file

papers.title: CrossCodeEval: A Diverse and Multilingual Benchmark for Cross-File Code Completion

notes.k: Multilingual cross-file completion benchmark
notes.r: Makes the cross-file dependency the measured unit, across languages

papers.title: RLCoder: Reinforcement Learning for Repository-Level Code Completion

notes.k: Trains the retriever with RL for repo-level completion
notes.r: Moves retrieval from heuristic to learned reward signal at repo scale

papers.title: EVOR: Evolving Retrieval for Code Generation

notes.k: Co-evolves the query and the knowledge base during generation
notes.r: Treats the retrieval target as moving, not fixed, during a task

papers.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?

notes.k: Issue-resolution benchmark over real GitHub repos
notes.r: The yardstick the agentic camp competes on, and the contamination-prone baseline the enterprise critique targets

papers.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering

notes.k: Purpose-built agent-computer interface for navigation/edit instead of a raw shell
notes.r: Sobering finding: iterative search can degrade performance versus no search at all

papers.title: OpenHands: An Open Platform for AI Software Developers as Generalist Agents

notes.k: Open agent platform generalizing the agent-computer interface
notes.r: The shared substrate much agentic-SE work builds and evaluates on

papers.title: AutoCodeRover: Autonomous Program Improvement

notes.k: AST-based search plus spectrum fault localization for autonomous repair
notes.r: Shows structural search beats flat retrieval for locating the edit site

papers.title: LocAgent: Graph-Guided LLM Agents for Code Localization

notes.k: Parses the codebase into a heterogeneous graph and does multi-hop reasoning over it
notes.r: 92.7% file-level localization, +12% issue resolution (Pass@10), ~86% cheaper with a fine-tuned 32B model

papers.title: ARISE: Repo-Level Graph and Toolset for Fault Localization and Program Repair

notes.k: Couples a repo-level graph with a toolset for fault localization and APR
notes.r: Extends graph-guided localization into the repair loop

papers.title: LingmaAgent: Towards Comprehensive Repository Exploration in Automated Software Engineering

notes.k: Monte-Carlo-style comprehensive repository exploration (Alibaba)
notes.r: Industrial agentic exploration strategy for unfamiliar repos

papers.title: CodePlan: Repository-Level Coding using LLMs and Planning

notes.k: Frames repo-wide edits as planning over a dependency graph
notes.r: Treats multi-file change as planning, not a chain of local completions

papers.title: AOCI: AI-Oriented Code Indexing for Repository-Scale Understanding

notes.k: Symbolic-semantic blueprint an LLM reads in a single pass; incrementally maintained
notes.r: Across 2,160 evals beats deployable baselines; zero defects on 19 industrial tasks vs 4-130x more tokens for mainstream agents

papers.title: QVoG: Scalable Defect Detection via Compressed Code-Property-Graph Traversal

notes.k: Compressed CPG with a declarative query language at 1M+ LOC
notes.r: Scalability proof: ~15 min vs ~19 min (CodeQL) on million-line codebases

papers.title: Vulnerability Detection using Code Property Graphs and CNNs

notes.k: CPG features fed to CNNs for vulnerability detection
notes.r: Code-property-graph lineage applied to security defect finding

papers.title: VulWeaver: Grounded Vulnerability Detection

notes.k: Grounds vulnerability detection in static analysis
notes.r: Static-analysis grounding to reduce false positives at scale

papers.title: Code Digital Twin: Representing and Maintaining Tacit Knowledge for Software Maintenance

notes.k: Co-evolving representation fusing knowledge graphs and text to capture tacit knowledge
notes.r: Argues the highest-value context (design rationale, responsibility) is what static analysis cannot extract

papers.title: Enabling Static Analysis Context for Repository-Level Coding Tools (IDE-Derived Context)

notes.k: Uses IDE/static-analysis-derived static context for coding tools
notes.r: Static-analysis retrieval supplying precise type/symbol context

papers.title: RealBench: Repository-Level Code Generation Aligned to Real-World Tasks

notes.k: Repo-level code-gen benchmark aligned to real-world tasks
notes.r: Pushes evaluation toward production realism

papers.title: Large Language Model-Based Agents for Software Engineering: A Survey

notes.k: Survey of LLM agents for software engineering
notes.r: Maps the agentic-SE landscape that retrieval underpins

papers.title: Agents in Software Engineering: Survey, Landscape, and Vision

notes.k: Survey and vision for agents in software engineering
notes.r: Complementary landscape framing for the agentic wave

papers.title: A^3-CodGen: Repository-Level Code Generation with Local/Global/Third-Party Awareness

notes.k: Names the retrieval-scope taxonomy: local-aware, global-aware, third-party-library-aware
notes.r: Splits repo-scale retrieval into three distinct problems, not one

papers.title: CodexGraph: Bridging Large Language Models and Code Repositories via Code Graph Databases

notes.k: Indexes the repo into a graph DB and lets the LLM write graph queries for context
notes.r: Reframes retrieval from vector-similarity guessing to querying a world model

papers.title: CodeGRAG: Bridging the Gap between Natural Language and Programming Language via Graphical Retrieval

notes.k: Builds control/data-flow graph views and trains a GNN expert to inject structure
notes.r: Pushes structural knowledge into the model rather than just the retriever

papers.title: MAGIS: LLM-Based Multi-Agent Framework for GitHub Issue Resolution

notes.k: Role decomposition with a dedicated 'Repository Custodian' for file localization
notes.r: Makes localization its own agent, separate from developer and QA

papers.title: LibEvolutionEval: A Benchmark for Library Evolution and Version-Specific Code Generation

notes.k: Tracks completion degradation as public-library APIs drift across versions
notes.r: Repo context is not just other files here; it is the moving external dependency surface, partly rescued by version-pinned docs

papers.title: Architecture Descriptors for Agentic Code Navigation

notes.k: Field study of 7,012 Claude Code sessions; a formal architecture descriptor cuts navigation 33-44%
notes.r: Undirected exploration is a measurable tax; descriptors slash navigation steps (d=0.92) and variance 52%; format choice should optimize safe failure, not LLM preference

papers.title: KGCompass: Repository-Aware Knowledge Graph for Bug Localization and Repair

notes.k: Repo-aware KG linking issues/PRs to code, narrowing to ~20 candidate functions
notes.r: 45.67% on SWE-bench-Lite at $0.20/repair; 69.7% of localized bugs needed multi-hop traversal, proof flat retrieval misses most fix sites

papers.title: R2C2-Coder: Enhancing and Benchmarking Real-world Repository-level Code Completion

notes.k: Real-world repo-level completion at monorepo scale
notes.r: Operates on the in-source half of enterprise context

papers.title: RepoQA: Evaluating Long Context Code Understanding

notes.k: Searching-Needle-Function over long code context; 500 tasks, 50 repos, 5 languages
notes.r: Capacity is not comprehension; models often understand code better with comments removed; GPT-4 only 1.31% in their setup

papers.title: CodeAgent: Enhancing Code Generation with Tool-Integrated Agent Systems for Repo-Level Coding

notes.k: Tool-integrated agent for repo-level coding challenges
notes.r: Brings external tools into the repo-scale coding loop

papers.title: RAMBO: Enhancing RAG-based Repository-Level Method Body Completion

notes.k: RAG-based repo-level method-body completion
notes.r: Scales the RAG completion pattern to method bodies in large repos

papers.title: RepoGenReflex: Enhancing Repository-Level Code Completion with Verbal RL and RAG

notes.k: Combines verbal RL (Reflexion-style) and RAG for multi-file completion
notes.r: Adds a reflective loop on top of multi-file retrieval

papers.title: On the Effectiveness of LLM Agents for Semantic Code Search

notes.k: LLM agents reformulate queries to improve semantic code search
notes.r: Agentic attack on the lossy intent→query hop

papers.title: MutaGReP: Execution-Free Repository-Grounded Plan Search

notes.k: Mutation-guided plan search grounded by a symbol retriever
notes.r: Plans using <5% of a 128K window rival GPT-4o with the full repo: retrieving a grounded plan beats stuffing the window

papers.title: Using a Nearest-Neighbour, BERT-Based Approach for Scalable Clone Detection

notes.k: NN/BERT approach for scalable clone detection
notes.r: Prerequisite for paying down clone/dup debt that accretes at enterprise scale

papers.title: Industrial-Scale Clone Detection with Disk-Based Similarity Search

notes.k: Disk-based similarity search for clone detection across millions of LOC
notes.r: Industrial-scale IR where in-memory approaches no longer fit

papers.title: Product Context Improves AI Coding Agent Decision Compliance by 49%

notes.k: Augmenting an agent with a product-context retrieval system lifts decision compliance 46%→95%
notes.r: Cleanest tribal-knowledge demonstration: 100% on codebase-visible decisions but 0-33% on product-context decisions without it

papers.title: K-Trans: Triple Knowledge-Augmentation for Repository-Context Code Translation

notes.k: Triple knowledge augmentation for polyglot repo-context translation
notes.r: +19.4%/40.2% pass@1; dependency-usage examples contribute most: the boundary, not the body, carries the hard context

papers.title: SWE-bench-java: A GitHub Issue Resolving Benchmark for Java

notes.k: Ports SWE-bench issue resolution to Java
notes.r: Exposes how much agentic-SE capability was Python-specific

papers.title: Enterprise-codebase challenge source (Episode 4 supporting reference)

notes.k: Supporting source for the enterprise-challenges episode; annotation unresolved in corpus
notes.r: Included because it is a real ep4 reference bibcode; title/abstract did not resolve via lexical-only lookup

papers.title: Enterprise-codebase challenge source (Episode 4 supporting reference)

notes.k: Supporting source for the enterprise-challenges episode; annotation unresolved in corpus
notes.r: Included because it is a real ep4 reference bibcode; title/abstract did not resolve via lexical-only lookup

papers.title: Enterprise-codebase challenge source (Episode 4 supporting reference)

notes.k: Supporting source for the enterprise-challenges episode; annotation unresolved in corpus
notes.r: Included because it is a real ep4 reference bibcode; title/abstract did not resolve via lexical-only lookup

papers.title: Enterprise-codebase challenge source (Episode 4 supporting reference)

notes.k: Supporting source for the enterprise-challenges episode; annotation unresolved in corpus
notes.r: Included because it is a real ep4 reference bibcode; title/abstract did not resolve via lexical-only lookup

papers.title: When Retrieval Hurts Code Completion: A Staleness Diagnostic

notes.k: Controlled diagnostic: hiding commit freshness makes stale-only retrieval inject obsolete APIs
notes.r: Stale context is net-negative (15/17 and 13/17 obsolete refs, +88/+77 pp); temporal validity is a distinct retrieval variable

papers.title: DocSync: Detecting and Repairing Code-Documentation Drift

notes.k: Failure taxonomy (Silent Constraint Shifts, Tutorial Rot) fixed by AST + RAG + a Reflexion critic
notes.r: Names doc drift that is 'functionally lethal' yet passes the linter; documentation staleness as a retrieval hazard

papers.title: GALA: Graph-Aligned Localization for Multimodal (GUI) Bug Resolution

notes.k: Builds a UI graph from bug screenshots and cross-references repo file/call graphs
notes.r: Extends graph-guided localization to visual→code grounding on SWE-bench Multimodal

papers.title: To Copilot and Beyond: A Survey of 860 Microsoft Developers on AI They Want Built

notes.k: Catalogues 22 wanted AI systems; central demand is 'bounded delegation'
notes.r: The design language for access-control and tribal knowledge: authority scoping, provenance, uncertainty, least privilege

papers.title: Multilingual Code Intelligence: A Survey

notes.k: Catalogs why cross-language generalization is hard and why deps are vital for buildability
notes.r: Polyglot-retrieval frame: retrieving class definitions plus their dependencies is vital for code buildability

papers.title: Breaking Changes in Software Ecosystems: A Systematic Literature Review

notes.k: SLR grounding dependency-graph propagation of breaking changes
notes.r: The dependency-drift angle that polyglot retrieval must track across an ecosystem

papers.title: Quantifying Contamination in Evaluating Code Generation Capabilities of Language Models

notes.k: Quantifies benchmark/pretraining overlap via surface- and semantic-level matching
notes.r: Models score higher on the seen subset of HumanEval/MBPP: the empirical case for contamination-proof eval

sections.label: Why Code != Text IR
sections.subtopic: Code retrieval is not text IR with a different corpus: query/code vocabulary mismatch, an open code lexicon, and meaning that lives in structure all break the assumptions of classical IR.
themes: Vocabulary mismatch: a developer's query and the target snippet often share almost no tokens (CodeSearchNet).
Open vocabulary: code coins identifiers endlessly, so OOV cripples neural LMs (Big Code != Big Vocabulary).
Structure is signal text IR throws away: data/control flow, call graphs (MISIM, GraphCodeBERT).
The intent→query→code chain is lossy at every hop, spawning a whole query-reformulation subfield.
Even metrics can't be borrowed: CodeBLEU re-injects AST + data flow that BLEU and exact-match miss.
Industry consensus: 'mgrep finds the file, grep finds the line' — no single mode suffices, everyone ends up hybrid.
how_measured: CodeSearchNet (2019): paired (docstring, function), 6 languages, MRR.
CoIR (2024): comprehensive multi-task multi-language code-IR suite, the modern successor.
CoSQA+ (2024): real developer web-search queries.
CodeBLEU (2020): AST + data-flow-aware synthesis metric (vs BLEU / exact-match).
gaps: Cross-paper retrieval scores (MRR on CodeSearchNet) are not apples-to-apples; preprocessing and language subsets differ — the fragmentation CoIR was built to fix.
Pre-2016 classic lexical/structural search (Sourcerer, Portfolio, Sniff, grep/ctags) is absent (pre-arXiv venues).
notable: CodeBERT is the seminal bimodal encoder and the token-level baseline its structure-aware successors react to.
CodeMatcher is the 'strong lexical baseline' that should temper any pure-neural pitch.

sections.label: Techniques: Lexical/Neural/Graph
sections.subtopic: An additive ladder, not a sequence of replacements: lexical owns rare tokens, dense embeddings own intent, graph methods own behavior, and cascades place each technique where its cost is justified.
themes: Rung 1 — lexical/trigram/BM25: fast, exact, interpretable, owns rare-token recall (Zoekt lineage, CodeMatcher).
Rung 2 — neural bi-encoders + contrastive learning: CodeBERT, ContraCode, CoCoSoDa; need hard negatives (RocketQA) or they underperform.
Rung 3 — AST/graph: data flow (GraphCodeBERT), flow graphs (deGraphCS), GNNs (GraphSearchNet), graph matching.
Rung 4 — hybrid retrieve-then-rerank / late interaction: multi-stage ranking (Lin et al.), ColBERT, Cascaded Fast & Slow, CoRNStack.
Fuse on ranks, not scores: BM25 and dense lanes live on incomparable scales; RRF sidesteps calibration.
Contriever is the receipt: unsupervised dense loses to BM25 — dense only wins with supervision.
how_measured: CodeSearchNet / CoIR as the standing benchmark spine; MRR on code search.
GraphCodeBERT: SOTA on code search, clone detection, translation, refinement.
ColBERT: parity with full BERT rerankers at ~2 orders faster, ~4 orders fewer FLOPs/query.
RocketQA / CoCoSoDa ablations attribute gains to hard-negative mining + augmentation.
gaps: Dense/ANN index was down during gathering — recall is lexical/hybrid only; CoRNStack metrics unverified via live lookup.
Graph methods are most expressive but cost parsing + GNN inference, hard to amortize across a multi-million-file monorepo.
notable: The trade curves matter more than leaderboard rows: single-vector vs late-interaction (index size), token vs graph (parse cost), unsupervised vs supervised dense (training data).
The 2026 industrial pipeline: Query → [BM25 top-20] + [Vector top-20] → RRF top-30 → cross-encoder rerank → top 5-10.

sections.label: Repository-Scale & Code Graphs
sections.subtopic: A file is a unit you read; a repository is a world you navigate. The field forks into retrieve-better-snippets (iterated similarity RAG) versus query-a-structured-model (code graphs), with agentic navigation walking between them.
themes: Camp 1 — similarity, iterated: RepoCoder's retrieve-generate-retrieve loop; DraCo steers it with dataflow.
Camp 2 — query a structured world model: CodexGraph (graph DB + LLM-written queries), CodeGRAG (GNN expert), AOCI (symbolic-semantic blueprint).
Code-property-graph / knowledge-graph lineage: QVoG at 1M+ LOC, Code Digital Twin for tacit knowledge.
Camp 3 — agentic navigation: SWE-agent's agent-computer interface, OpenHands, MAGIS, LocAgent, CodePlan, ARISE.
Localization is a first-class sub-problem; flat similarity misses fix sites several call/import hops from the symptom.
Retrieval scope is three problems: local-aware, global-aware, third-party-library-aware (A^3-CodGen).
how_measured: RepoBench, CrossCodeEval: repo-level / cross-file completion (EM, identifier-F1).
SWE-bench: issue-resolution (Pass@k) the agentic camp competes on.
LocAgent: 92.7% file-level localization, +12% Pass@10, ~86% cheaper with a fine-tuned 32B.
QVoG: ~15 min vs ~19 min (CodeQL) at 1M+ LOC; AOCI: 2,160 evals + 19 industrial tasks, zero defects.
gaps: Graphs cost a static-analysis build step + language-specific extractors + index maintenance; similarity RAG is cheaper and language-agnostic.
Referenced-but-uncorpus: RepoGraph (ICLR 2025), GRACE multi-semantic code graphs (arXiv:2509.05980) — the next reading.
Many results are small-N arXiv preprints (Jin n=24 localization tasks; AOCI industrial slice n=19).
notable: Industrial primitives are the ancestors of the academic 'code knowledge graph': Kythe (build-integrated), Glean (incremental Angle index), SCIP (cross-repo precise nav), Zoekt (trigram).
Jin 2026: across S-expr/JSON/YAML/Markdown there is no comprehension difference — pick descriptor format by safe-failure mode, not LLM preference.

sections.label: Enterprise Codebase Challenges
sections.subtopic: Scale does not just make existing problems bigger — it changes which problems exist: billions of LOC, polyglot dependency chains, access boundaries, dead code, tribal knowledge, build complexity, and an evaluation crisis where ground truth is contaminated or tautological.
themes: Sheer size: Google ~2B LOC, 9M files, ~40K commits/day — query-time linear search is infeasible.
Escalation ladder: grep → trigram (Zoekt) → semantic index (Kythe) → incremental build-integrated (Glean) → cross-repo precise nav (SCIP). Text layer and semantic layer are routinely conflated.
Polyglot: defects hide at the language boundary; dependency-usage context (K-Trans) carries the hard-to-retrieve knowledge.
Tribal/proprietary knowledge: the 'why' lives in a person or Slack thread; product context lifts decision compliance 46%→95%.
Access-control boundaries: 'find references' that returns unreadable code is a leak — the thinnest academic sub-topic.

Local benchmark projects encode the real enterprise tasks: EnterpriseBench (multi-repo nav, tool-access-as-variable, dead_code_necropsy), CodeScaleBench (benchmarks the retrieval engines, not just LLMs), codeprobe (contamination-proof probes, multi-source consensus).
how_measured: Published EnterpriseBench (arXiv:2510.27287): 550 tasks, access-control hierarchies, SOTA ~21.5% (best agents fail ~4/5).
RepoQA: Searching-Needle-Function, 500 tasks / 50 repos / 5 languages; GPT-4 1.31% setup pass rate.
MutaGReP: plans use <5% of a 128K window yet rival full-repo GPT-4o (LongCodeArena).
Local CodeScaleBench (Haiku 4.5): mean reward 0.536 baseline → 0.565 MCP — real but modest.
Local EnterpriseBench: 112 tasks, 51% spanning 2-5 real OSS repos via real dependency chains; checkpoint partial-credit, 9 artifact validators.
codeprobe: multi-source consensus (Sourcegraph + AST oracle + grep, ship only when >=2 agree) + bias detectors (backend_overlap, overshipping, no_independent_baseline).
gaps: Access-control / retrieval-under-permission-boundaries is near-absent academically (only published EnterpriseBench + Sourcegraph compliance framing).
Industrial systems (Kythe/Zoekt/Glean/SCIP) are blog-grounded, not peer-reviewed.
Benchmark numbers collapse on production: Passerine reached ~73% plausible on machine-reported but only ~25.6% plausible / 17.9% correct on human-reported Google bugs.
notable: The ground-truth tautology: if you generate the 'right answer' with the same tool you are evaluating, you have measured nothing (codeprobe).
Treat tool access as a controlled variable; always report a no-tool baseline — a tool that does not beat baseline is overhead (EnterpriseBench / CodeScaleBench).
RepoQA finding: models often understand code better with comments removed — synthetic comments are a distractor.

sections.label: Frontier & Open Problems
sections.subtopic: The bottleneck is no longer 'can a model write a function' but an infrastructure problem: put fresh, authorized, dependency-correct, build-grounded context in front of an agent inside a living monorepo.
themes: Staleness-aware retrieval: stale context is net-negative — gate retrieval on freshness, not just relevance (Weng; DocSync).
Incremental real-time indexing: reframe indexing cost from O(repository) to O(changes) (Glean) — almost no peer-reviewed work, industry holds the frontier.
Graph-guided edit localization: 69.7% of correctly localized bugs need multi-hop KG traversal (KGCompass); GALA extends to multimodal GUI bugs.
Symbolic+semantic fusion at monorepo scale: nobody ships the fused, always-fresh index — the unclaimed prize (AOCI, QVoG vs Kythe/SCIP/Zoekt/Glean).
Access-control & tribal-knowledge retrieval: 'bounded delegation' with least-privilege scoping is what 860 developers asked for (To Copilot and Beyond).
Honest evaluation: contamination (Riddell) and tool-vs-no-tool causality undermine benchmark numbers unless explicitly controlled.
how_measured: Staleness: stale-only retrieval → obsolete-API refs 15/17 (Qwen2.5-Coder-7B), 13/17 (gpt-4.1-mini), +88.2/+76.5 pp vs current-only; no-retrieval passed 1/17.
Localization: KGCompass 45.67% on SWE-bench-Lite at $0.20/repair.
Tribal knowledge: +49 pp decision compliance from product context.
Contamination: Riddell shows models score significantly higher on the seen subset of HumanEval/MBPP.
gaps: Permission-aware retrieval and tribal-knowledge retrieval are the thinnest academically; enforcement leans on industry (PermLLM arXiv:2505.22860, Access Control for LLM Agents arXiv:2510.11108).
Long-context-vs-retrieval policy is genuinely open and thin in this corpus (Gemini 1.5 long-context referenced but absent); RepoQA is the needle-in-haystack foil.
Incremental indexing has almost no peer-reviewed coverage.
notable: The defining bet: the first system that ships a fused symbolic+semantic index that stays fresh under O(changes) updates wins this frontier.
Retrieval can hurt — Weng's result is direct dissent against RAG-maximalist orthodoxy and argues for retrieval gating.

reading: CodeBERT: A Pre-Trained Model for Programming and Natural Languages
CodeSearchNet Challenge: Evaluating Semantic Code Search
SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
GraphCodeBERT: Pre-training Code Representations with Data Flow
Big Code != Big Vocabulary: Open-Vocabulary Models for Source Code
CodeMatcher: Lexical Search Rivaling Neural Code Search
CoIR: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Code Information Retrieval
LocAgent: Graph-Guided LLM Agents for Code Localization
Product Context Improves Agent Decision Compliance by 49%
opportunities: Retrieval under access-control boundaries

Make permission a first-class retrieval feature, not a post-hoc filter: 'find references' must respect what the caller is allowed to read, or it leaks. The thinnest academic sub-topic; enforcement leans on PermLLM-style permissioned LLMs and dynamic ACLs for agentic information flows.
Contamination-proof eval from private history

Public benchmarks (HumanEval, SWE-bench) are memorized; Riddell quantifies the seen-subset advantage. Mine contamination-proof probes from private/own repo history (codeprobe) instead of trusting memorized public sets.
Symbolic+semantic hybrid index at monorepo scale

Symbolic indexes (Kythe/SCIP) are precise but build-coupled; semantic embeddings are cheap but imprecise on exact references. Nobody ships the fused, always-fresh index — the unclaimed prize (AOCI, QVoG are the corpus anchors).
Staleness-aware retrieval

Carry commit timestamps / version pins into the index and decline stale context rather than serving it — Weng shows serving stale context is worse than serving nothing (obsolete-API refs in 15/17 samples).
Polyglot dependency-aware retrieval

Cross-language defects hide at the boundary; retrieving class definitions plus their dependencies is vital for code buildability (K-Trans: dependency-usage examples contribute most; Multilingual Code Intelligence survey).
Tribal-knowledge retrieval

The 'why' of a decision lives in people, Slack threads, design docs — never in source. A separate retrieval substrate for decisions/specs/personas lifted compliance 46%→95%; it is retrievable and measurably changes behavior.
Build-graph-grounded context selection
What even is the dependency graph is a question the build system answers; ground context selection in the build graph (Kythe/Glean are build-integrated) rather than text similarity over files.
Tool-vs-no-tool causal evaluation

The field lacks a clean isolation of 'did the tool help, or did the model already know?' Always report a no-tool baseline and isolate the tool variable (CodeScaleBench engines comparison; codeprobe MCP-vs-no-MCP isolation modes).
Long-context vs retrieval policy

When does a million-token full-repo dump beat selective retrieval? Capacity is not comprehension (RepoQA); a <5%-of-window grounded plan rivals full-repo GPT-4o (MutaGReP). The honest open question is the crossover point.
Incremental real-time index maintenance
Reframe indexing from O(repository) to O(changes): a 40K-commits/day monorepo cannot be fully re-indexed per change. Glean is the production exemplar; peer-reviewed work is nearly absent.
Graph-guided edit localization

Flat similarity structurally misses bug-fix sites: 69.7% of correctly localized bugs needed multi-hop KG traversal (KGCompass). Build a language-agnostic, incrementally-updatable repository graph before scaling embeddings.
Cost-latency-bounded retrieval at scale

Latency and UX are first-order at enterprise scale (Meta CodeCompose). Design retrieval under explicit cost/latency budgets — a cascade that spends the expensive technique only on a short pre-pruned list.

design.title: CodeSearchNet → CoIR: the code-IR benchmark family
design.task_design: NL→code retrieval evaluated as ranking the correct function for a natural-language query. CodeSearchNet pairs (docstring, function) across 6 languages and was built explicitly around the query/code vocabulary gap; CoIR generalizes to a comprehensive multi-task, multi-language code-IR suite consolidating a fragmented landscape into one benchmark.
design.data_generation: CodeSearchNet harvests docstring/function pairs from open-source repos; CoIR aggregates and standardizes many code-IR tasks (text-to-code, code-to-code, retrieval-augmented) so models are compared on one preprocessing regime. CoSQA+ adds genuine developer web-search queries to escape synthetic-docstring bias.
design.metrics: Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) / nDCG / recall@k over a candidate pool; CoIR reports per-task and aggregate scores across languages.
key_numbers: CoIR consolidates a multi-task multi-language suite (the modern successor)
Cross-paper CodeSearchNet MRR is not apples-to-apples — the exact problem CoIR fixes
design.reusable_for_role: Evaluation harness designers: use CoIR (not raw CodeSearchNet MRR) as the standing spine for comparing retrievers, and CodeBLEU-style structural metrics for synthesis — never plain BLEU or exact-match, which silently punish semantically-correct outputs.

design.title: LocAgent: graph-guided code localization
design.task_design: Given a GitHub issue, localize the file(s)/function(s) to edit by parsing the codebase into a directed heterogeneous graph (files/classes/functions as nodes; imports/invocations/inheritance as edges) and letting the agent do multi-hop reasoning over it, rather than flat similarity retrieval.
design.data_generation: Localization tasks derived from real issue-resolution benchmarks; graph constructed by static parsing of the repository, so it is language-agnostic and incrementally updatable.
design.metrics: File-level localization accuracy; downstream issue-resolution success (Pass@10); cost per task.
key_numbers: ~86% lower cost with a fine-tuned Qwen-2.5-Coder-32B vs proprietary SOTA
Corroborated by KGCompass: 69.7% of localized bugs need multi-hop traversal
design.reusable_for_role: Agent-infra builders: make localization its own measurable component (cf. MAGIS's Repository Custodian), build a repository graph before scaling embeddings, and traverse it with multi-hop reasoning to reach fix sites that are not lexically near the symptom.

design.title: EnterpriseBench (local): multi-repo navigation with tool-access as a controlled variable
design.task_design: Coding-agent navigation of large, distributed, MULTI-REPO enterprise codebases. 112 tasks / 10 task types / 7 workflow suites (Dependency Mgmt, Customer Escalation, Technical Debt, Feature Delivery, Incident Response, Platform Eng, Security Ops). Encodes monorepo boundary navigation, dead/stale code (dead_code_necropsy task type), polyglot dependency complexity, and config drift. Crucially makes tool access a controlled variable: grep/find vs Sourcegraph-MCP vs hybrid.
design.data_generation: 51% of tasks span 2-5 real OSS repos via real dependency chains (grpc-go→etcd→kubernetes; protobuf→Go→Python→TS). Distinct from the published arXiv:2510.27287 namesake. Refuses to assume the agent can see everything.
design.metrics: Checkpoint partial-credit scoring with 9 artifact validators; contrasts SWE-bench / ITBench / DevOps-Gym; always reports a no-tool baseline.
key_numbers: 51% of tasks span 2-5 real OSS repos
Published EnterpriseBench (arXiv:2510.27287) SOTA ~21.5% — best agents fail ~4/5 enterprise tasks
design.reusable_for_role: Enterprise eval designers: treat tool access as a controlled variable and always report a no-tool baseline (a tool that doesn't beat baseline is overhead); use checkpoint partial-credit and artifact validators instead of single pass/fail; pair with codeprobe's multi-source consensus to defeat the ground-truth tautology.

design_principles: Default to hybrid, not embeddings: treat exact/lexical match as a first-class lane and fuse with a dense retriever on ranks (RRF), not raw scores.
Add a structural lane where you can parse the language: AST/data-flow signals (GraphCodeBERT/MISIM) pay off on symbol-level and cross-file queries; degrade to lexical+dense on files that don't parse.
Spend the expensive technique only on the shortlist: a cheap first stage prunes the corpus; a cross-encoder or ColBERT-style late interaction reranks the top 20-30.
Mine hard negatives or your embeddings underperform (RocketQA, CoCoSoDa) — the retriever is only as good as its negatives.
Index, don't just embed: a static-analysis graph (CodexGraph) or symbolic-semantic blueprint (AOCI) answers multi-hop structural questions vector RAG cannot.
Make localization its own component, separate from patch generation (MAGIS, LocAgent).
Give the agent a map before it explores: front-load architecture context; undirected grep/glob is a measurable tax and search can backfire.
Gate retrieval on freshness and permissions, not just relevance: stale or unauthorized context is worse than none.
Treat tool access as a controlled variable in evals and always report a no-tool baseline.
Budget for the half of the truth not in the source: a separate retrieval substrate for tribal/product knowledge can outweigh another point of code-search recall.
design_pitfalls: Shipping 'semantic code search' as embeddings-only and being surprised it can't find OAuth2TokenValidator — embeddings miss rare exact identifiers that grep nails.
Fusing lexical and dense lanes on raw scores; BM25 and cosine live on incomparable scales — fuse on ranks.
Evaluating code retrieval/synthesis with text metrics (BLEU, exact-match), which punish semantically-correct outputs — use CodeBLEU and a real code-IR suite.
Conflating the trigram text layer (Zoekt: where does this string appear) with the semantic layer (Kythe/SCIP: where is this symbol defined) — they answer different questions.
Full-reindexing a 40K-commits/day monorepo: a stale index silently degrades every agent downstream; incremental indexing is architecture, not optimization.
Serving stale retrieval results: under hidden freshness, retrieval was net-negative and injected obsolete APIs (Weng).
Undirected exploration: more searching is not more finding — SWE-agent shows iterative search can degrade performance vs no search.
Choosing descriptor formats by presumed LLM preference rather than safe-failure mode (JSON fails atomically, YAML silently corrupts 50%, S-expressions detect all structural errors).
Trusting public-benchmark numbers as production capability — they collapse on human-reported enterprise work (Passerine ~25.6% vs ~73% machine-reported).
The ground-truth tautology: generating the 'right answer' with the same tool you're evaluating measures nothing — require multi-source consensus (>=2 of {Sourcegraph, AST oracle, grep}).

publication_volume.note: Deep API Learning / DeepCS lineage — learning the NL→API mapping before the encoder era.
CodeSearchNet establishes the foundational NL→code retrieval benchmark.
The CodeBERT explosion: bimodal encoders, GraphCodeBERT data flow, ColBERT late interaction, MISIM, CodeMatcher, CodeBLEU, RocketQA — the technique ladder forms.
Contrastive + graph rungs mature (Contriever, CodeT5, deGraphCS, GraphSearchNet); query reformulation review.
Unified/cross-modal embeddings (UniXcoder, CoCoSoDa), survey consolidation, large-scale text+code contrastive.
Repo-scale arrives: RepoCoder, RepoBench, CrossCodeEval, SWE-bench, CodePlan, A^3-CodGen — retrieval becomes navigation.
The agentic + graph + RAG wave: SWE-agent, OpenHands, CodexGraph, DraCo, QVoG, CoIR, RepoQA, MutaGReP plus the enterprise-completion cluster.
Graph-guided localization and knowledge graphs (LocAgent, KGCompass, Code Digital Twin, K-Trans); industrial-scale clone detection.
Frontier: staleness diagnostics, doc-drift, multimodal graph localization, symbolic-semantic indexing, access-control/tribal-knowledge framing, contamination-aware eval.

benchmark_progression: 2019: CodeSearchNet — the foundational NL→code retrieval yardstick (MRR, 6 languages).
2020: CodeBLEU re-grounds synthesis evaluation in AST + data flow.
2023: RepoBench / CrossCodeEval make cross-file completion measurable; SWE-bench makes real issue resolution the agentic yardstick.
2024: CoIR consolidates code-IR eval; RepoQA tests long-context understanding; CodeRAG-Bench isolates whether retrieval helps.

2025-2026: localization-specific (KGCompass, LocAgent), staleness diagnostics (Weng), contamination-aware (Riddell), and enterprise sandboxes (published EnterpriseBench ~21.5% SOTA) — the bars move from 'retrieve from a visible repo' to 'navigate a repo you can't fully see, freshly and honestly'.
temporal.narrative: The field matured along one arc: borrow text IR, discover it breaks on code, then climb a technique ladder (lexical → neural → graph → rerank) before scaling from file to repository to enterprise. Citations concentrate in the 2020 encoder wave (CodeBERT, GraphCodeBERT) and the 2023 SWE-bench pivot to issue resolution, but the volume center of gravity is the 2024 agentic/graph/RAG explosion. By 2026 the frontier is explicitly an infrastructure problem — freshness, access-control, multi-hop graph localization, and honest tool-causal evaluation — where industry (Kythe, Glean, SCIP, Zoekt) still leads the peer-reviewed literature.
